actions.value:
I am sorry to say but our engineers confirmed the last publicly reachable Bitcoin Gold node was operated by Trezor, and it’s now offline. That means:

  1. No Peer Connections
    With zero BTG nodes to connect to, Edge can’t query any blockchain data—no balances, no transaction history, no chain tips.
  2. Cannot Broadcast Transactions
    Even if you craft a valid BTG transaction, there’s nowhere to send it. Without at least one node, it simply won’t propagate.
  3. Security & Maintenance
    Spinning up and maintaining your own BTG node is both resource-intensive and risky given the project’s lack of active development. Any unpatched vulnerability could jeopardize users’ funds.

In short:
Bitcoin Gold’s network is effectively down. Edge Wallet can’t support a chain that has no active nodes to talk to, so even if we wanted to, there’s no way for us—or you to use it. 

 

It's an unfortunate situation for the community. Please let us know if you have any other questions we are happy to help.
